open_hooks_to_carry: Ren's ship lifts off from Dry Well — the crew watches; Spike says 'She didn't ask us to do anything' and then 'That's the worst part'
The quiet Bebop flight back — Spike naps, Faye rereads, Jet flies, Ed games
prev_tail_excerpt:
Still.

A family had weight in the hours behind it. Old jokes. Old hunger. The exact plate Faye stole from because Spike would put up the least fight. The exact tone Jet used when he was pretending not to be pleased. The way Ed leaned into the center of the room without checking whether there was space because of course there was. Ren had found the buried lines that made this crew possible long before they met. She had not lived the days that turned possibility into habit.

Ed looked up from his plate with sauce on his chin.

"Ren's breakfast wins," he declared.

Jet straightened. "Hold on."

"Data is clear."

"Your data is usually sticky."

"Still clear."

Faye lifted her mug in a lazy salute toward Ren. "I'm voting with the side that brought breakfast."

Spike shrugged. "Seems fair."

Jet looked around the room like a man measuring the scale of a betrayal and finding it domestic instead of fatal.

"Unbelievable," he said.

But he took another piece anyway.

Ren looked down at her plate so they wouldn't see the expression cross her face and decide it needed comment. It wasn't envy. Not exactly. Envy wanted possession. This was recognition with nowhere to go. The sight of a thing from the far side of glass. Warm. Real. Not hers.

She ate while they complained about tea, argued about pepper, and fed the dog when Jet wasn't looking.
state_deltas_required: The question of what changes when you cannot unknow something — return to equilibrium with permanent alteration
Return to the original status quo with everything underneath it changed; the found family is reaffirmed as chosen rather than accidental; domesticity continues, now understood
